  
  

![](reference/figures/icon.gif)

  
  

###   FielDHub

  
  

### Overview

FielDHub is an R package/shiny design of experiments (DOE) app that aids
in the creation of traditional, un-replicated, augmented and
partially-replicated designs applied to agriculture, plant breeding,
forestry, animal and biological sciences.

For more details and examples of all functions present in the FielDHub
package. Please, go to
<https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/index.html>.

![](reference/figures/FielDHub_Infographic.jpg)

## Usage

This is a basic example which shows you how to launch the app:

``` r
library(FielDHub)
run_app()
```

### Diagonal Arrangement Example

A project needs to test 280 genotypes in a field containing 16 rows and
20 columns of plots. In this example, these 280 genotypes are divided
among three different experiments. In addition, four checks are included
in a systematic diagonal arrangement across experiments to fill 40 plots
representing 12.5% of the total number of experimental plots. An option
to include filler plots is also available for fields where the number of
experimental plots does not equal the number of available field plots.

![](reference/figures/DExample.PNG)

The figure above shows a map of an experiment randomized along with
multiple experiments (three) and checks on diagonals. Distinctively
colored check plots are replicated throughout the field in a systematic
diagonal arrangement.

![](reference/figures/multiple_expts.png)

The figure above shows the layout for the three experiments in the
field.

### Using the FielDHub function `diagonal_arrangement()`

To illustrate using FielDHub to build experimental designs through R
code, the design produced in the R Shiny interface described above can
also be created using the function
[`diagonal_arrangement()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/diagonal_arrangement.md)
in the R script below. Note, that to obtain identical results, users
must include the same random seed in the script as was used in the Shiny
app. In this case, the random seed is 1249.

``` r
diagonal <- diagonal_arrangement(
  nrows = 16, 
  ncols = 20, 
  lines = 280, 
  checks = 4, 
  plotNumber = 101, 
  splitBy = "row", 
  seed = 1249, 
  kindExpt = "DBUDC", 
  blocks = c(100, 100, 80), 
  exptName = c("Expt1", "Expt2", "Expt3")
)
```

Users can print the returned values from
[`diagonal_arrangement()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/diagonal_arrangement.md)
as follow,

``` r
print(diagonal)
Un-replicated Diagonal Arrangement Design 

Information on the design parameters: 
List of 11
 $ rows          : num 16
 $ columns       : num 20
 $ treatments    : num [1:3] 100 100 80
 $ checks        : int 4
 $ entry_checks  :List of 1
  ..$ : int [1:4] 1 2 3 4
 $ rep_checks    :List of 1
  ..$ : num [1:4] 10 10 10 10
 $ locations     : num 1
 $ planter       : chr "serpentine"
 $ percent_checks: chr "12.5%"
 $ fillers       : num 0
 $ seed          : num 1249

 10 First observations of the data frame with the diagonal_arrangement field book: 
   ID  EXPT LOCATION YEAR PLOT ROW COLUMN CHECKS ENTRY TREATMENT
1   1 Expt1        1 2023  101   1      1      0    42    Gen-42
2   2 Expt1        1 2023  102   1      2      0    23    Gen-23
3   3 Expt1        1 2023  103   1      3      0    10    Gen-10
4   4 Expt1        1 2023  104   1      4      0    45    Gen-45
5   5 Expt1        1 2023  105   1      5      0    51    Gen-51
6   6 Expt1        1 2023  106   1      6      0    13    Gen-13
7   7 Expt1        1 2023  107   1      7      3     3   Check-3
8   8 Expt1        1 2023  108   1      8      0    43    Gen-43
9   9 Expt1        1 2023  109   1      9      0    84    Gen-84
10 10 Expt1        1 2023  110   1     10      0   102   Gen-102
```

First 12 rows of the fieldbook,

``` r
head(diagonal$fieldBook, 12)
   ID  EXPT LOCATION YEAR PLOT ROW COLUMN CHECKS ENTRY TREATMENT
1   1 Expt1        1 2023  101   1      1      0    42    Gen-42
2   2 Expt1        1 2023  102   1      2      0    23    Gen-23
3   3 Expt1        1 2023  103   1      3      0    10    Gen-10
4   4 Expt1        1 2023  104   1      4      0    45    Gen-45
5   5 Expt1        1 2023  105   1      5      0    51    Gen-51
6   6 Expt1        1 2023  106   1      6      0    13    Gen-13
7   7 Expt1        1 2023  107   1      7      3     3   Check-3
8   8 Expt1        1 2023  108   1      8      0    43    Gen-43
9   9 Expt1        1 2023  109   1      9      0    84    Gen-84
10 10 Expt1        1 2023  110   1     10      0   102   Gen-102
11 11 Expt1        1 2023  111   1     11      0    89    Gen-89
12 12 Expt1        1 2023  112   1     12      0    75    Gen-75
```

Users can plot the layout design from
[`diagonal_arrangement()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/diagonal_arrangement.md)
using the function
[`plot()`](https://rdrr.io/r/graphics/plot.default.html) as follows,

``` r
plot(diagonal)
```

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-5-1.png)

In the figure, salmon, green, and blue shade the blocks of unreplicated
experiments, while distinctively colored check plots are replicated
throughout the field in a systematic diagonal arrangement.

The main difference between using the `FielDHub` Shiny app and using the
standalone function
[`diagonal_arrangement()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/diagonal_arrangement.md)
is that the standalone function will allocate filler only if it is
necessary, while in Shiny App, users can customize the number of fillers
if it is needed. In cases where users include fillers, either between or
after experiments, the Shiny app is preferable for filling and
visualizing all field plots.

To see more examples, go to
<https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/diagonal_arrangement.html>

### Partially Replicated Design Example

Partially replicated designs are commonly employed in early generation
field trials. This type of design is characterized by replication of a
portion of the entries, with the remaining entries only appearing once
in the experiment. As an example, considered a field trial with 288
plots containing 75 entries appearing two times each, and 138 entries
only appearing once. This field trials is arranged in a field of 16 rows
by 18 columns.

![](reference/figures/prep_shiny.png)

In the figure above, green plots contain replicated entries, and the
other plots contain entries that only appear once.

### Using the FielDHub function `partially_replicated()`

Instead of using the Shiny FielDHub app, users can use the standalone
FielDHub function
[`partially_replicated()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/partially_replicated.md).
The partially replicated layout described above can be produced through
scripting as follows. As noted in the previous example, to obtain
identical results between the script and the Shiny app, users need to
use the same random seed, which, in this case, is 77.

``` r
pREP <- partially_replicated(
  nrows = 16, 
  ncols = 18,  
  repGens = c(138,75),
  repUnits = c(1,2),
  planter = "serpentine", 
  plotNumber = 1,
  exptName = "ExptA",
  locationNames = "FARGO",
  seed = 77
)
```

Users can print returned values from
[`partially_replicated()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/partially_replicated.md)
as follows,

``` r
print(pREP)
Partially Replicated Design 


 Replications within location: 
  LOCATION Replicated Unreplicated
1    FARGO         75          138

 Information on the design parameters: 
List of 7
 $ rows             : num 16
 $ columns          : num 18
 $ min_distance     : num 8
 $ incidence_in_rows: num 3
 $ locations        : num 1
 $ planter          : chr "serpentine"
 $ seed             : num 77

 10 First observations of the data frame with the partially_replicated field book: 
   ID  EXPT LOCATION YEAR PLOT ROW COLUMN CHECKS ENTRY TREATMENT
1   1 ExptA    FARGO 2023    1   1      1     30    30       G30
2   2 ExptA    FARGO 2023    2   1      2      0   192      G192
3   3 ExptA    FARGO 2023    3   1      3     44    44       G44
4   4 ExptA    FARGO 2023    4   1      4     66    66       G66
5   5 ExptA    FARGO 2023    5   1      5      0    78       G78
6   6 ExptA    FARGO 2023    6   1      6      0   186      G186
7   7 ExptA    FARGO 2023    7   1      7     34    34       G34
8   8 ExptA    FARGO 2023    8   1      8      0    86       G86
9   9 ExptA    FARGO 2023    9   1      9     37    37       G37
10 10 ExptA    FARGO 2023   10   1     10     55    55       G55
```

First 12 rows of the fieldbook,

``` r
head(pREP$fieldBook, 12)
   ID  EXPT LOCATION YEAR PLOT ROW COLUMN CHECKS ENTRY TREATMENT
1   1 ExptA    FARGO 2023    1   1      1     30    30       G30
2   2 ExptA    FARGO 2023    2   1      2      0   192      G192
3   3 ExptA    FARGO 2023    3   1      3     44    44       G44
4   4 ExptA    FARGO 2023    4   1      4     66    66       G66
5   5 ExptA    FARGO 2023    5   1      5      0    78       G78
6   6 ExptA    FARGO 2023    6   1      6      0   186      G186
7   7 ExptA    FARGO 2023    7   1      7     34    34       G34
8   8 ExptA    FARGO 2023    8   1      8      0    86       G86
9   9 ExptA    FARGO 2023    9   1      9     37    37       G37
10 10 ExptA    FARGO 2023   10   1     10     55    55       G55
11 11 ExptA    FARGO 2023   11   1     11      0   125      G125
12 12 ExptA    FARGO 2023   12   1     12      0   159      G159
```

Users can plot the layout design from
[`partially_replicated()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/partially_replicated.md)
using the function
[`plot()`](https://rdrr.io/r/graphics/plot.default.html) as follows,

``` r
plot(pREP)
```

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-9-1.png)

To see more examples, please go to
<https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/partially_replicated.html>

  
  

# Package index

## All functions

- [`CRD()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/CRD.md)
  : Generates a Completely Randomized Design (CRD)

- [`RCBD()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/RCBD.md)
  : Generates a Randomized Complete Block Design (RCBD)

- [`RCBD_augmented()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/RCBD_augmented.md)
  : Generates an Augmented Randomized Complete Block Design (ARCBD)

- [`alpha_lattice()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/alpha_lattice.md)
  : Generates an Alpha Design

- [`diagonal_arrangement()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/diagonal_arrangement.md)
  : Spatial Un-replicated Diagonal Arrangement Design

- [`do_optim()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/do_optim.md)
  : Generate the sparse or p-rep allocation to multiple locations.

- [`full_factorial()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/full_factorial.md)
  : Generates a Full Factorial Design

- [`incomplete_blocks()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/incomplete_blocks.md)
  : Generates a Resolvable Incomplete Block Design

- [`latin_square()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/latin_square.md)
  : Generates a Latin Square Design

- [`multi_location_prep()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/multi_location_prep.md)
  : Optimized multi-location partially replicated design

- [`optimized_arrangement()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/optimized_arrangement.md)
  : Generates an Spatial Un-replicated Optimized Arrangement Design

- [`partially_replicated()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/partially_replicated.md)
  : Generates a Spatial Partially Replicated Arrangement Design

- [`plot(`*`<FielDHub>`*`)`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/plot.FielDHub.md)
  :

  Plot a `FielDHub` object

- [`print(`*`<FielDHub>`*`)`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/print.FielDHub.md)
  :

  Print a `FielDHub` object

- [`print(`*`<fieldLayout>`*`)`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/print.fieldLayout.md)
  :

  Print a `fieldLayout` plot object

- [`print(`*`<summary.FielDHub>`*`)`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/print.summary.FielDHub.md)
  :

  Print the summary of a `FielDHub` object

- [`rectangular_lattice()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/rectangular_lattice.md)
  : Generates a Rectangular Lattice Design.

- [`row_column()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/row_column.md)
  : Generates a Resolvable Row-Column Design (RowColD)

- [`run_app()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/run_app.md)
  : Run the Shiny Application

- [`sparse_allocation()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/sparse_allocation.md)
  : Unreplicated designs using the sparse allocation approach

- [`split_families()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/split_families.md)
  : Split a population of genotypes randomly into several locations.

- [`split_plot()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/split_plot.md)
  : Generates a Split Plot Design

- [`split_split_plot()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/split_split_plot.md)
  : Generates a Split Split Plot Design

- [`square_lattice()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/square_lattice.md)
  : Generates a Square Lattice Design.

- [`strip_plot()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/strip_plot.md)
  : Strip Plot Design

- [`summary(`*`<FielDHub>`*`)`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/summary.FielDHub.md)
  :

  Summary a `FielDHub` object

- [`swap_pairs()`](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/reference/swap_pairs.md)
  : Swap pairs in a matrix of integers

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Alpha Lattice
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/alpha_lattice.md):
- [Completely Randomized
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/crd.md):
- [Unreplicated Diagonal Arrangement
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/diagonal_arrangement.md):
- [UNKNOWN
  TITLE](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/news/fieldhub-0-1-0.md):
- [UNKNOWN
  TITLE](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/news/fieldhub-1-2-0.md):

  TITLE](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/news/fieldhub-1-3-1.md):
- [Full Factorial
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/full_factorial.md):
- [Incomplete Block
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/incomplete_blocks.md):
- [Latin Square
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/latin_square.md):
- [Methods in
  FielDHub](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/methods.md):
- [Optimized Multi-Location P-rep
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/multi_location_prep.md):
- [Un-replicated Optimized Arrangement
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/optimized_arrangement.md):
- [Partially Replicated (p-rep)
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/partially_replicated.md):
- [Augmented Randomized Complete Block
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/RCBD_augmented.md):
- [Randomized Complete Block
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/rcbd.md):
- [Rectangular Lattice
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/rectangular_lattice.md):
- [Row-Column
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/row_column.md):
- [Sparse
  Allocation](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/sparse_allocation.md):
- [Split-Plot
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/split_plot.md):
- [Split-Split Plot
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/split_split_plot.md):
- [Square Lattice
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/square_lattice.md):
- [Strip-Plot
  Design](https://didiermurillof.github.io/FielDHub/articles/strip_plot.md):
